postData.contentHtml:
Areas of Ireland where people say "aye"

A few weeks ago a friend from down the country pointed out that I say "aye" to mean "yes" a lot and I couldn't stop noticing every time I say it. Is this something from my local Monaghan dialect or is it something I picked up from working Belfast? I did some research but couldn't find anything conclusive.

So I asked reddit ireland where are they from and whether or not they say the word "aye". The thread has 287 comments so I thought it would be fun to map it out.

When you map it out here we have a pretty nice "aye" isogloss: people in Ulster (and Sligo...and Wicklow for some reason) say "aye", other people generally don't.

I can finally say aye without thinking about it again.
postData.title: Areas of Ireland where people say "aye"
postData.short: Mapping out the aye/yes isogloss in Ireland
